key_events: Earth is splitting open beneath the Pacific Northwest, scientists say
Scientists finally explain how the Twelve Apostles rose from the ocean
Hidden ocean heat is creeping toward Antarctica’s fragile ice shelves

raw_items.title: Earth is splitting open beneath the Pacific Northwest, scientists say
raw_items.summary: For the first time, scientists have watched a subduction zone literally fall apart beneath the ocean floor. Using advanced seismic imaging, they found the Juan de Fuca plate splitting into fragments as it sinks beneath North America. Rather than collapsing all at once, the plate is tearing piece by piece, like a train slowly derailing. The finding helps explain ancient plate fragments and could refine how scientists understand earthquake behavior.

raw_items.title: Scientists finally explain how the Twelve Apostles rose from the ocean
raw_items.summary: Australia’s famous Twelve Apostles didn’t just erode into existence—they were slowly pushed up from the ocean floor by powerful tectonic forces over millions of years, new research reveals. Scientists discovered that these towering limestone stacks act like a natural time capsule, preserving clues about ancient climates, sea levels, and even life from up to 14 million years ago.

raw_items.title: Hidden ocean heat is creeping toward Antarctica’s fragile ice shelves
raw_items.summary: Deep beneath the Southern Ocean, a quiet but alarming shift is underway: warm water is creeping closer to Antarctica, and scientists are now seeing it clearly for the first time. By combining decades of ship data with robotic float measurements and machine learning, researchers uncovered that a massive pool of heat—circumpolar deep water—has expanded and edged toward the continent over the past 20 years.

raw_items.title: Scientists just found a chilling way life may have begun
raw_items.summary: New experiments suggest that freezing and thawing on early Earth may have helped primitive cell-like structures grow and evolve. Tiny lipid bubbles behaved very differently depending on their membrane makeup—some fused into larger compartments and captured DNA more efficiently. These fusion events could have mixed key molecules, setting the stage for more complex chemistry.

raw_items.title: Geoengineering
raw_items.summary: Geoengineering (also known as climate engineering or climate intervention) is the deliberate large-scale interventions in the Earth’s climate system intended to counteract human-caused climate change. The term commonly encompasses two broad categories: large-scale carbon dioxide removal (CDR) and solar radiation modification (SRM). raw_items.title: Planetary boundaries
raw_items.summary: Planetary boundaries are a framework to describe limits to the impacts of human activities on the Earth system. Beyond these limits, the environment may not be able to continue to self-regulate.
